texto: Anecdotal tales of combat are meaningless to Americans, we absorb tales of violence like a sponge. Mythological violence is second nature to us. The real thing is not. War begins long before battle. It begins when we are boys longing for the initiation rite of the warrior and everything it promises: sexual prowess and sexual license. War lasts long after the last bullet is fired; into old age and death we go carrying a secret knowledge that no one wants to know about. War is the opposite of sexual prowess. War is desire stripped of humanity.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Alfredo Véa: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de temas ligados a reflexao. O trecho “Anecdotal tales of combat are meaningless to Americans, we absorb tales of violence like a sponge…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, war, sexual pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de luto e reflexao.

War begins long before battle. It begins when we are boys longing for the initiation rite of the warrior and everything it promises: sexual prowess and sexual license. War lasts long after the last bullet is fired; into old age and death we go carrying a secret knowledge that no one wants to know about. War is the opposite of sexual prowess. texto: I must have been chosen because I know it all from beginning to end. I am certainly not the story itself. I am only the grass that tattles on the wind.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Alfredo Véa sobre esforço, persistência e resultados. A afirmação “I must have been chosen because I know it all from beginning to end. I am certainly not the story…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a sucesso, gods-go-begging, stories, útil em momentos de termino e stories.
